title: Math — Linear Equations (One Variable)
description: SAT-style one-variable linear equation questions, including distribution, combining like terms, and modeling with rates and costs.

questions.prompt: If \(2(x-5)+3(x-5)=10\), what is the value of \(x-5\)?
Megan earns \(p\) dollars per hour for the first 8 hours of work and \(1.5p\) for each hour beyond 8. If she worked 10 hours and earned \($137.50\), what is her regular hourly wage?
questions.explanation: First 8 hours: \(8p\). Extra 2 hours: \(2(1.5p)=3p\). Total: \(11p=137.5\Rightarrow p=12.5\).

questions.prompt: Alan drives 100 miles per week. His car averages 25 miles per gallon and gas costs \($4\) per gallon. Which equation can be used to find how many fewer miles \(m\) he must drive to reduce his weekly gas cost by \($5\)?
questions.explanation: Cost per mile is \(\frac{4}{25}\) dollars (\($4\) per gallon ÷ 25 miles per gallon). A reduction of \(m\) miles saves \(\frac{4}{25}m\) dollars. Set savings to 5: \(\frac{4}{25}m=5\).

questions.prompt: If \(x\) is the solution to \(3(2x-6)-11=4(x-3)+6\), what is the value of \(x-3\)?
A product sells for \($100\) and the salesperson earns a 20% commission. If the company makes \($6{,}840\) profit, which equation gives the number of units \(u\) sold?
questions.explanation: Company keeps \(100(1-0.2)=80\) dollars per unit after commission. If cost is 65 per unit, profit per unit is \(80-65=15\). Total profit: \(15u=6840\), which is \((100(1-0.2)-65)u=6840\).

questions.prompt: A bin initially contains 24,000 bushels of corn. After 5 hours, 19,350 bushels remain. If corn is removed at a constant rate, how many hours after starting will 12,840 bushels remain?
questions.explanation: Removed in 5 hours: \(24000-19350=4650\), so rate is \(4650/5=930\) bushels/hour. Model: \(24000-930t=12840\Rightarrow930t=11160\Rightarrow t=12\).

questions.prompt: If \(2(3t-10)+t=40+4t\), what is the value of \(3t\)?
In the equation \(4a+12=a(8+a)+3\), \(a\) is a constant. If the equation has infinitely many solutions, what is the value of \(a\)?
questions.explanation: For infinitely many solutions, both sides must be identical after simplifying (same coefficients and constants). Solving the identity condition gives \(a=8\).

questions.prompt: How many solutions does the equation \(12(z-3)=-3(z+12)\) have?
